mechanism_narrative: Sustained near-zero federal funds rate compresses risk-free rates and equity discount rates, mechanically raising the present value of future corporate cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in the majority of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window, accelerating labor market slack depresses consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts generate a non-linear positive wealth effect on equities because simultaneous consumption and residential investment recovery reduces recession probability more than either variable alone, amplifying S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint interaction term of RSAFS and HOUST delivers a rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal underperforms both single-proxy benchmarks.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns only when monetary policy is accommodative (FEDFUNDS below 1.5%), because low financing costs allow supply-side expansion to translate into margin expansion rather than inflationary pressure that prompts tightening.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS is below 1.5%, month-over-month INDPRO growth fails to positively predict 20-day SPX returns with rank IC exceeding 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in CPI inflation exceeding 0.3 percent signals impending erosion of real corporate earnings or future monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of instances when the CPI trigger is activated.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirm a genuine labor market recovery rather than a participation-rate distortion, jointly predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease produces a rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent signals acute supply-side contraction and impending earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals expanding aggregate demand and supports corporate revenue growth, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth coincides with negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of quarters.
